adminIntegrations.title: Integrations
adminIntegrations.subtitle: Real-time status of all external services
adminIntegrations.restartingDesc: You will be logged out. Wait for the services to come back up and log in again.

adminConfig.title: System Settings
adminConfig.subtitle: Values stored in the database. Secrets are never displayed.
adminConfig.newSecretPlaceholder: Enter new value (will not be shown)
adminConfig.restartRequired: This setting requires a container restart to take effect.
adminConfig.emptyValueDesc: Please provide a value to save.
adminConfig.restartWarning: Settings marked with "Requires restart" will only take effect after restarting the API container. Use the "Restart & Apply" button to apply the changes and update the .env.

restartModal.title: Restart & Apply Settings
restartModal.desc: Choose the execution environment. Settings saved in the database will be applied on restart. You will be logged out automatically.

LLM_PROVIDER.hint: Defines which provider the agent system uses. Switching between providers requires a container restart.

OLLAMA_BASE_URL.description: HTTP address of the Ollama server.
OLLAMA_BASE_URL.hint: HTTP address of the Ollama server. Inside Docker Compose, the service name 'ollama' resolves automatically. For Ollama installed on the host (outside Docker), use http://host.docker.internal:11434.

OLLAMA_MODEL.description: Ollama model tag for text generation (chat).
OLLAMA_MODEL.hint: Ollama model tag for text generation (chat). The model must be downloaded beforehand. Recommended for development: mistral:7b-instruct (works well with 16 GB RAM).

OLLAMA_EMBED_MODEL.hint: Ollama model for generating vector embeddings (RAG). Must be aligned with vectors already stored in pgvector — switching models without re-indexing produces poor search results.

LLM_TIMEOUT_CHAT.description: Maximum seconds to wait for a chat response.
LLM_TIMEOUT_CHAT.hint: Maximum seconds to wait for a chat response. On hardware without GPU or with large models, increase to 300 or more to avoid timeouts.

LLM_TIMEOUT_EMBED.description: Maximum seconds to wait for an embedding.
LLM_TIMEOUT_EMBED.hint: Maximum seconds to wait for an embedding. Embeddings are generally faster than chat; rarely needs to be increased.

ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.description: API key for use with Claude (required if LLM_PROVIDER=anthropic).
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.hint: Anthropic API secret key. Required when LLM_PROVIDER=anthropic. Obtained at console.anthropic.com → API Keys. Stored encrypted in the database.
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.warning: Never commit this key to code. It is only displayed once in the Anthropic Console when created.

ANTHROPIC_MODEL.hint: Claude model ID to use. IDs are versioned; see docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/about-claude/models for the latest recommended ID.

EMAIL_BACKEND.description: Django email backend Python class. Use SMTP in production.
EMAIL_BACKEND.hint: Django email backend Python class. In production, use the SMTP backend. In development, the 'console' backend prints emails to the container stdout instead of sending them.

EMAIL_HOST.hint: SMTP server hostname. Examples by provider: Gmail → smtp.gmail.com, Outlook → smtp.office365.com, SES → email-smtp.<region>.amazonaws.com, SendGrid → smtp.sendgrid.net.

EMAIL_PORT.description: SMTP port. Use 587 with TLS, 465 with SSL.
EMAIL_PORT.hint: SMTP port. Use 587 with STARTTLS (EMAIL_USE_TLS=True), 465 with implicit SSL (EMAIL_USE_SSL=True), or 25 without encryption (not recommended in production).

EMAIL_USE_TLS.hint: Enables STARTTLS. Use with port 587. Never set EMAIL_USE_TLS=True and EMAIL_USE_SSL=True at the same time — this will cause a connection error.
EMAIL_USE_TLS.warning: Do not enable simultaneously with EMAIL_USE_SSL=True.

EMAIL_HOST_USER.description: SMTP authentication user (usually the email address).
EMAIL_HOST_USER.hint: SMTP authentication user — usually the full email address. For SendGrid, the value is always the literal word 'apikey'.

EMAIL_HOST_PASSWORD.description: SMTP password. For Gmail with 2FA, use an App Password.
EMAIL_HOST_PASSWORD.hint: SMTP password. Stored encrypted. For Gmail with 2-step verification, use an App Password (myaccount.google.com/apppasswords) — not your Google account password. For SendGrid, use the API key (starts with SG.).
EMAIL_HOST_PASSWORD.warning: For Gmail, create a specific App Password. The Google account password does not work when 2-step verification is active.

DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL.description: Address displayed in the 'From' field of sent emails.
DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL.hint: Address displayed in the 'From' field of sent emails. The address must be authorized by the SMTP provider to avoid emails being marked as spam.

SITE_URL.description: Public frontend URL, used for links in emails.
SITE_URL.hint: Public URL of the frontend, used to generate clickable links in emails (e.g., password reset link). Must include the protocol (https://).

BACKUP_CRON.description: Cron expression for automatic backup scheduling.
BACKUP_CRON.hint: Cron expression for automatic database backup scheduling. Requires a backup service restart to take effect after the change.

KEEP_DAILY.description: Number of daily backups to keep.
KEEP_DAILY.hint: Number of daily backups to keep. Older backups are automatically deleted by the retention service.

KEEP_WEEKLY.description: Number of weekly backups to keep.
KEEP_WEEKLY.hint: Number of weekly backups to keep (one per ISO week). Complements daily retention for older data recovery.

KEEP_MONTHLY.description: Number of monthly backups to keep.
KEEP_MONTHLY.hint: Number of monthly backups to keep. Recommended to keep at least 3 months for compliance and disaster recovery.

BACKUP_ENCRYPTION_KEY.description: AES-256 password for encrypting backup files.
BACKUP_ENCRYPTION_KEY.hint: AES-256 password for encrypting backup files. Independent from the application ENCRYPTION_KEY — they are distinct keys with different purposes.
BACKUP_ENCRYPTION_KEY.warning: Changing this key invalidates decryption of all previous backups. Save the current value before switching.

DEBUG.description: Django debug mode. Never enable in production.
DEBUG.hint: Activates Django debug mode. In debug mode, full tracebacks are shown in error responses and production optimizations are disabled.
DEBUG.warning: Never use True in production — it exposes sensitive server information in error responses.

LOG_FORMAT.hint: 'json' is structured and integrable with tools like Grafana/Loki. 'verbose' is readable in the terminal and recommended for development.

BUDGET_ENFORCEMENT_MODE.hint: Behavior when a configured budget is exceeded. 'soft' allows the operation and returns a warning (HTTP 201). 'hard' blocks the operation with an error (HTTP 400).

ALLOWED_HOSTS.hint: Domains accepted by Django, comma-separated. Requests with a Host header not listed are rejected with HTTP 400. In development: localhost,127.0.0.1.

CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS.hint: Origins allowed for CORS requests, comma-separated. Must include the frontend URL. In development: http://localhost:39101.

GUNICORN_WORKERS.hint: Number of Gunicorn worker processes. General rule: 2 × CPU_cores + 1. More workers increase throughput but also RAM usage.

SECRET_KEY.description: Django secret key for signing sessions and tokens.
SECRET_KEY.hint: Django secret key used to sign sessions, CSRF tokens, and cookies. Must be long, random, and unique per environment.
SECRET_KEY.warning: Changing this key immediately invalidates all active sessions — all users will be logged out. Generate a new one with: docker compose exec api python -c "from django.core.management.utils import get_random_secret_key; print(get_random_secret_key())"

ENCRYPTION_KEY.description: Fernet key for encrypting sensitive database fields.
ENCRYPTION_KEY.hint: Fernet key (44 chars base64) for encrypting sensitive fields in the database: Account._account_number, CreditCard._card_number/_security_code, Member._document, among others.
ENCRYPTION_KEY.warning: Never change this key directly. Use the management command 'rotate_encryption_key' with --old-key and --new-key. Changing without rotation makes all encrypted data unreadable and unrecoverable. See documentation for the complete procedure.

MINIO_ENDPOINT.hint: Internal MinIO address (host:port). Inside Docker Compose, the service name 'minio' resolves automatically. For external access (outside Docker), use localhost:39105.

MINIO_BUCKET_NAME.description: Name of the main bucket for media storage.
MINIO_BUCKET_NAME.hint: Name of the main bucket for media storage. The bucket is automatically created on first startup if it doesn't exist. Use only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens.

MINIO_ROOT_USER.hint: MinIO root user, equivalent to the MINIO_ROOT_USER variable defined in docker-compose. Must match the value configured in the MinIO service.

MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD.hint: MinIO root password. Stored encrypted in the database. Must match the password configured in the MinIO service. The MinIO Console is available at http://localhost:39106.
MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD.warning: This password must match the one configured in the MinIO service. A mismatch causes authentication failure and makes file storage inaccessible.
